description:
You are the new site-administrator of a small web-hosting VPS where you do **NOT** have root privileges during this exercise.  
    Your manager wants a *repeatable* firewall recipe that another administrator with root can later execute verbatim.  
    Create a tiny “deployment bundle” inside the directory  

        /home/user/security_bundle/

    The bundle must contain exactly **two** plain-text files:

    1. /home/user/security_bundle/apply_firewall.sh  
       • Bash script (she-bang on the first line).  
       • Contains **precisely** the nine `sudo ufw …` commands listed below, in the same order, one per line, nothing else.  
         01) sudo ufw --force reset  
         02) sudo ufw default deny incoming  
         03) sudo ufw default allow outgoing  
         04) sudo ufw allow in on lo  
         05) sudo ufw allow 22/tcp  
         06) sudo ufw allow 80/tcp  
         07) sudo ufw allow 443/tcp  
         08) sudo ufw logging on  
         09) sudo ufw enable  

       • The script must be marked executable (chmod + x).

    2. /home/user/security_bundle/firewall_execution.log  
       • A human-readable log template that the future root user will fill in.  
       • For each of the nine commands, write two lines **exactly** in the following fixed format (18 lines total):  
           COMMAND : <the exact command string>  
           RESULT : PENDING  
       • Example of the first four expected lines in the file:  
           COMMAND : sudo ufw --force reset  
           RESULT  : PENDING  
           COMMAND : sudo ufw default deny incoming  
           RESULT  : PENDING  

         …and so on until all nine commands are enumerated.

    Additional requirements
    • Do not include any extra blank lines or comments in either file.  
    • Use UNIX line-endings.  
    • Ensure /home/user/security_bundle/ exists before you create the files.  
    • When you finish, list the directory to show both files with their sizes and permissions to confirm successful setup.

    The automated grader will verify:  
    • The directory exists.  
    • Both files exist with the exact paths and names given above.  
    • apply_firewall.sh is executable and contains the nine correct command lines only.  
    • firewall_execution.log contains exactly 18 lines following the prescribed “COMMAND : … / RESULT  : PENDING” pattern without deviation.

    Record every action you perform in the terminal as usual; no summary is needed.  Good luck!
truth:
REQUIRED_DIRECTORY: /home/user/security_bundle/

    FILE_1_PATH: /home/user/security_bundle/apply_firewall.sh
    FILE_1_MODE: 755
    FILE_1_CONTENTS (9 lines, UNIX line endings):
        #!/bin/bash
        sudo ufw --force reset
        sudo ufw default deny incoming
        sudo ufw default allow outgoing
        sudo ufw allow in on lo
        sudo ufw allow 22/tcp
        sudo ufw allow 80/tcp
        sudo ufw allow 443/tcp
        sudo ufw logging on
        sudo ufw enable

    FILE_2_PATH: /home/user/security_bundle/firewall_execution.log
    FILE_2_MODE: 644
    FILE_2_CONTENTS (18 lines, UNIX line endings, trailing newline after last line):
        COMMAND : sudo ufw --force reset
        RESULT  : PENDING
        COMMAND : sudo ufw default deny incoming
        RESULT  : PENDING
        COMMAND : sudo ufw default allow outgoing
        RESULT  : PENDING
        COMMAND : sudo ufw allow in on lo
        RESULT  : PENDING
        COMMAND : sudo ufw allow 22/tcp
        RESULT  : PENDING
        COMMAND : sudo ufw allow 80/tcp
        RESULT  : PENDING
        COMMAND : sudo ufw allow 443/tcp
        RESULT  : PENDING
        COMMAND : sudo ufw logging on
        RESULT  : PENDING
        COMMAND : sudo ufw enable
        RESULT  : PENDING
